block_01: Festivities, schedules, and hints for promotion in Asia and Africa
block_03: From the previous email, you’ve learned how people celebrate Christmas and New Year in Europe and Latin America. This time, we’ll focus on our top-performing countries of Asia and Africa in terms of FTD.
block_04: Asian and African traditions are very different from what we see in the Western Christmas movies. Use our hints to tweak your promo materials to the local holiday spirit!
block_07: Asian countries gladly adopt the commercialized side of the Christmas holidays such as decorations and save their party fuel for the winter holidays of their own. Here are the most significant ones in the countries from our list:
block_08: India: India Republic Day (January 26), The Hindu festival of Thaipusam (January 28), Maha Shivratri Day (February 21)
block_10: Philippines: Rizal Day (December 30), Lunar new year (February 12)
block_11: Vietnam: Lunar new year (February 12), Vietnamese New Year (January 24-25)
block_12: Thailand: Thailand Сonstitution Day (December 10), King Bhumibol’s Birthday (December 5), Lunar new year (January 25)
block_13: Hint: When markets are closed for the holidays, promote trading on the OTC (over-the-counter) market. Point out the opportunity to trade over the days-off when the market is less crowded.
block_15: The Christian community in Africa is big, so Christmas is celebrated on a grand scale. People travel to their villages to visit relatives, gather up on the patios for a festive dinner, sing carols, share gifts, or go to the beach. New Year is the time of massive parties, masquerades, and dancing.
block_16: Hint: In hot Africa, many "winter" attributes are replaced by local counterparts. However, the overall idea of ​​the holiday is not much different from the Western tradition.
block_17: Sprinkle your promotions with the local holiday vibes and have a prosperous end of the year!
